channel.title: NullSheen - Tools for Shadowrun (And more) on NullSheen - TTRPG Gaming Tools and Reviews
channel.description: Recent content in NullSheen - Tools for Shadowrun (And more) on NullSheen - TTRPG Gaming Tools and Reviews

item.title: Mythic Bastionlands
item.description: It’s been awhile my friends! For me its been quite a few busy months of adjusting to a new job and seeing where things fall for a lot of different things in my life. One thing that of course has stayed the same is my ravenous appetite for new and interesting games. I have quite a banger for you this time too. A Fantasy Game Unlike Any Other Mythic Bastionlands is like nothing I have ever played or run before.

item.title: Gunslinger Knights Review
item.description: Im a big fan of a lot of games (Which is why I have this website honestly), that goes double for games with unique settings and very few are as unique as Gunslinger Knights. It’s a game that is a bit weird but wholly wonderful. Part post-apocalypse, part 1980s cartoon, part Arthurian legend, it holds something for everyone who has even the slightest inclination for high octane fun. The Elevator Pitch Gunslinger Knights is a game about dimension hopping Arthurian Knights traveling across a medieval world on magical motorcycles fighting for a pacifist people against necromantic agents of chaos who have started to invade a world destroyed by a magical apocalypse.

item.title: Gunslinger Knights
item.description: Gunslinger Knights Welcome to the Gunslinger Knights section! This page will feature reviews and information about this exciting Cypher System RPG. About Gunslinger Knights Where to Purchase Preview Game (Free) - PDF - DriveThruRPG Full Game - PDF - DriveThruRPG Physical Book - Monte Cook Games Reviews Game Review

item.title: Mythic Bastionland
item.description: Mythic Bastionland is a fantasy RPG of myth-building and exploration, where you play as Knights protecting Bastionland from mythic threats while building your own legends. 🎬 Trailer 🎲 Tools & Resources Mythic Bastionland tools and resources are in development. 📚 Useful Links Mythic Bastionland - Official announcement Bastionland - Chris McDowall’s blog mythic.bastionland.tools - Map Maker - This is by far the best map maker. Want more Mythic Bastionland content? Let me know!

item.title: Public Access
item.description: Public Access is a post modern horror game about a group of people (The Latchkeys) returning to their home town to investigate an old TV Station called “TV Odyssey”. Rules are based on Powered by the Powered by the Apocalypse, but heavily modified in how those rules are applied. This is a game you don’t want to miss. 🎲 Tools & Resources Public Access tools and resources are in development.

item.title: Dungeons & Dragons
item.description: The world’s greatest roleplaying game, from classic Advanced Dungeons & Dragons 2nd Edition to modern 5th Edition. 📖 Adventure Reviews Dive into reviews of classic and modern adventures, from the golden age of AD&D 2nd Edition to the latest 5th Edition releases. AD&D 2nd Edition Classic adventures from the TSR era - coming soon! D&D 5th Edition Modern adventures and campaign reviews - coming soon! 🎲 Tools & Resources D&D tools and resources are in development.

item.title: Old Gods of Appalachia - Folk Magic Overview
item.description: This is my attempt to make a kind of unified folk magic primer for new people coming to the game that want to play wise witch and cunning folk. This is definitely a work in progress and doesn’t cover everything thats possible out there, but its a good place to start perhaps. If you guys have any input, please send it my way! -D Nailing it Down / Protection Magic Description: People want to keep what’s theirs: their house, their land, their people.

item.title: Live Streams
item.description: Watch live TTRPG sessions, tool development, and gaming content on Twitch! Join the community for Shadowrun, Heart, and other tabletop adventures. 🔴 Live Stream Follow on Twitch 💬 Live Chat 📺 Recent Streams & Highlights View All Past Streams → What to Expect TTRPG Sessions Shadowrun campaigns and one-shots Heart: The City Beneath delves Other tabletop RPG systems Character creation and worldbuilding Tool Development Live coding of web tools Feature requests and bug fixes Community feedback sessions Behind-the-scenes development Gaming & Discussion TTRPG news and reviews System discussions Q&A sessions Community hangouts Stream Schedule Check the Twitch channel for the current schedule!

item.title: Fallout Status Tracker
item.description: Track status effects, conditions, and combat for the Fallout RPG using the Modiphius 2d20 system. Manage radiation, injuries, and all the hazards of the wasteland. ☢️ Launch Fallout Tracker Features Status Effect Tracking - Monitor all active conditions Radiation Exposure - Track rads and radiation sickness Injury Management - Record wounds and their effects Combat Tracking - Initiative and action point management Condition Monitoring - Health, hunger, thirst, and fatigue Party Management - Track multiple characters How to Use Add Characters - Create entries for PCs and NPCs Track Status - Apply conditions and effects Monitor Radiation - Record rad exposure and effects Manage Combat - Track initiative and actions Update Conditions - Modify as situations change Clear Effects - Remove conditions when resolved What is Fallout 2d20?

item.title: Hex Crawler
item.description: Generate and explore hex-based wilderness maps for your tabletop RPG campaigns. Perfect for sandbox games, exploration, and procedural world-building. 🗺️ Launch Hex Crawler Features Hex Map Generation - Create wilderness hex maps Terrain Types - Forests, mountains, plains, swamps, and more Point of Interest - Generate locations and encounters Travel Tracking - Monitor party movement across hexes Random Encounters - Generate events during travel Export Maps - Save and share your hex crawls How to Use Generate Map - Create a hex-based wilderness area Set Terrain - Define terrain types for each hex Add Locations - Place settlements, dungeons, and landmarks Track Travel - Monitor party position and movement Roll Encounters - Generate random events Explore - Reveal hexes as players discover them Perfect For Sandbox Campaigns - Open-world exploration Wilderness Adventures - Travel between locations West Marches Style - Player-driven exploration Procedural Content - Generate on-the-fly Campaign Mapping - Visualize your world Terrain Types Forests and woodlands Mountains and hills Plains and grasslands Swamps and marshes Deserts and badlands Rivers and lakes Roads and trails Settlements and ruins Hex Crawl Concepts Exploration Each hex represents a region to explore Travel takes time and resources Discovering new hexes reveals content Map fills in as players explore Encounters Random encounters during travel Planned locations in specific hexes Environmental challenges Resource management Scale Adjust hex size for your campaign 6-mile hexes (classic D&D) 12-mile hexes (larger scale) Custom sizes for your needs Tips for GMs Prepare key locations in advance Use random generation for filler Track weather and seasons Consider travel time and supplies Reward exploration with discoveries Connect hexes with roads and rivers System Agnostic Works with any tabletop RPG:

item.title: Shadowrun Weapon Designer
item.description: Design custom weapons for Shadowrun 3rd Edition with balanced stats and costs. Create unique firearms, melee weapons, and exotic armaments for your campaigns. 🔫 Launch Weapon Designer Features Custom Weapon Creation - Design firearms, melee, and exotic weapons Balanced Stats - Automatic cost calculation based on stats Modification System - Add accessories and modifications Damage Calculation - Power, staging, and recoil values Availability Rating - Determine street availability Export Options - Save and share your designs How to Use Choose Weapon Type - Firearm, melee, or exotic Set Base Stats - Damage, range, and rate of fire Add Modifications - Scopes, silencers, smartlinks, etc.

item.title: Heart RPG Delve Planner
item.description: Plan your delves into the nightmare dungeon beneath Spire with the Heart RPG Delve Planner. Track landmarks, domains, stress, and fallout as your characters descend into the cursed depths. 🖤 Launch Heart Planner Features Delve Tracking - Map your journey through the Heart Domain Management - Track which domains you’ve explored Landmark Database - Reference landmarks and their effects Stress & Fallout - Monitor character deterioration Resistance Tracking - Keep tabs on resistance levels Session Notes - Document your descent How to Use Create Your Delve - Start a new expedition into the Heart Add Characters - Track your party members Map Domains - Record which domains you’ve visited Track Stress - Monitor stress levels across resistances Record Fallout - Document the consequences of delving Plan Routes - Chart your path through the nightmare What is Heart?

item.title: Initiative Tracker
item.description: Track initiative for your Shadowrun 2nd and 3rd Edition games with ease. Manage multiple combatants, track condition monitors, and handle initiative passes automatically. ⚔️ Launch Initiative Tracker Features Edition Toggle - Switch between 2nd and 3rd Edition rules Automatic Initiative Passes - Handles multiple passes correctly Condition Monitors - Track physical and stun damage Damage Integration - Initiative automatically reduces as characters take damage Add/Remove Combatants - Manage NPCs and PCs on the fly Initiative Rolls - Roll for all combatants or enter manually How to Use Select Edition - Choose SR2 or SR3 rules Add Combatants - Enter names and initiative scores Roll Initiative - Let the tracker roll or enter values manually Track Combat - Click through initiative passes Apply Damage - Update condition monitors as needed Edition Differences 2nd Edition Base 10 initiative system Initiative passes at 10-second intervals 3rd Edition Base 10 initiative system Multiple initiative passes based on initiative score Automatic initiative reduction with damage Perfect For Game Masters - Keep combat flowing smoothly Online Games - Share your screen for remote play New GMs - Let the tool handle the math Fast Combat - No more manual tracking Tips Add all combatants before starting combat Use the damage toggle to automatically reduce initiative Keep the tracker visible to all players for transparency Pre-roll NPC initiatives to save time Future Features Save/load combat encounters Custom initiative modifiers Sound effects and notifications Mobile-optimized interface Works with: Shadowrun 2nd Edition, Shadowrun 3rd Edition

item.title: Matrix Builder
item.description: Design detailed Matrix systems for Shadowrun 2nd Edition with complete control over every node, IC, and connection. Perfect for GMs who want custom-built systems for their runs. 🔧 Launch Matrix Builder Features Visual Node Editor - Drag and drop interface for building systems Custom IC Placement - Choose exactly where each IC goes Connection Management - Define how nodes connect Security Ratings - Set individual node security levels Save/Load Systems - Build a library of reusable systems Export Options - Print or share your designs How to Use Create Nodes - Add nodes to your system Connect Nodes - Draw connections between nodes Add IC - Place IC in strategic locations Set Security - Configure security ratings Test & Refine - Review your system layout Save - Export for use in your game Perfect For Detailed Prep - Build complex systems for major runs Unique Locations - Create memorable Matrix environments Teaching - Show players how Matrix systems work Reusability - Build once, use multiple times Design Tips Start with the paydata location and work backwards Place IC at chokepoints and critical nodes Consider multiple entry points for variety Balance challenge with player capabilities Add flavor with node descriptions Edition: Shadowrun 2nd Edition

item.title: Matrix Security Sheaf Generator
item.description: Generate complete Matrix security systems for Shadowrun 3rd Edition on the fly. Create realistic corporate hosts, government systems, and private networks with appropriate IC and security measures. 🖥️ Launch Matrix Generator Features Instant Generation - Create complete security sheafs in seconds Security Ratings - Generate systems from Rating 1 to 12+ IC Placement - Appropriate IC types for security level System Architecture - Realistic node layouts Customizable - Adjust generated systems to fit your needs Print-Friendly - Export for use at the table How to Use Set Security Rating - Choose the system’s security level Generate System - Click to create a complete sheaf Review Layout - Check nodes, IC, and access points Customize - Adjust as needed for your run Save/Print - Keep for your game session What Gets Generated System Topology - Node structure and connections IC Distribution - Black, Gray, and White IC placement Access Points - Entry nodes and security checkpoints Datastore Locations - Where the paydata lives Security Protocols - Alert levels and response procedures Perfect For Busy GMs - Quick prep for decker runs Improvisation - Generate systems on the fly Learning - See how Matrix systems should be structured Consistency - Balanced systems every time System Types Generate appropriate systems for:

item.title: Rifts Initiative Tracker
item.description: Track initiative for your Rifts RPG combat encounters. Manage multiple combatants, handle actions per melee, and keep your battles flowing smoothly. ⚔️ Launch Rifts Initiative Tracker Features Actions Per Melee - Track multiple attacks per round Initiative Order - Automatic sorting by initiative roll Add/Remove Combatants - Manage NPCs and PCs dynamically Action Tracking - Mark actions as they’re used Round Counter - Keep track of melee rounds How to Use Add Combatants - Enter names and initiative rolls Set Actions - Input attacks per melee for each combatant Track Combat - Click through actions and rounds Manage Participants - Add or remove as needed Reset - Start fresh for the next encounter Perfect For Rifts GMs - Keep complex combats organized Online Games - Share screen for remote play Large Battles - Manage many combatants easily New GMs - Let the tool handle the bookkeeping Rifts Combat Basics Rifts uses a unique combat system where:

item.title: Shadowrun Character Generator
item.description: Create your Shadowrun 2nd and 3rd Edition characters with our web-based character generator. Build street samurai, deckers, mages, and more with an intuitive interface that handles all the math for you. 🎲 Launch Character Generator Features Priority System - Classic Shadowrun character creation 2nd & 3rd Edition Support - Toggle between editions Automatic Calculations - Karma costs, skill points, and attribute limits Equipment Selection - Browse and purchase gear, weapons, and cyberware Character Export - Save and print your character sheets Beta Version - Actively being developed with new features How to Use Choose Your Edition - Select Shadowrun 2nd or 3rd Edition Set Priorities - Assign priorities to Race, Magic, Attributes, Skills, and Resources Build Your Character - Allocate points and select equipment Review & Export - Check your character and export when ready Supported Editions Shadowrun 2nd Edition - Full priority system support Shadowrun 3rd Edition - Complete character creation rules Tips for New Players Start with a concept (street samurai, decker, mage, etc.

item.title: Shadowrun Run Generator
item.description: Need inspiration for your next Shadowrun? Generate complete run ideas with objectives, complications, and twists. Perfect for GMs who need quick prep or want to improvise. 💼 Launch Run Generator Features Complete Run Ideas - Generate full run concepts instantly Multiple Run Types - Extraction, datasteal, wetwork, and more Complications - Add twists to keep players on their toes Johnson Types - Different employers with different agendas Payment Options - Nuyen, favors, or other compensation Randomize Everything - Or pick specific elements What Gets Generated Run Type - The core objective Target - Who or what the runners are after Location - Where the run takes place Opposition - Who’s trying to stop them Complications - Plot twists and challenges Payment - What the Johnson is offering Run Types Include Extraction (person or object) Data theft Wetwork (assassination) Protection detail Sabotage Investigation Courier runs And many more!

item.title: Rifts RPG
item.description: Rifts is the legendary post-apocalyptic RPG from Palladium Books where magic and technology collide across dimensional rifts. Play as anything from cyber-knights to dragon hatchlings in a world gone mad. 🎲 Tools & Resources Initiative Tracker Rifts Initiative Tracker - Track initiative for your Rifts combat encounters with ease More Rifts tools coming soon! 📚 Useful Links Palladium Books - Official publisher Rifts Product Line - Browse Rifts books and supplements Savage Rifts - Rifts for Savage Worlds system 🛒 Get Rifts Books Looking for Rifts books?

item.title: Heart: The City Beneath
item.description: Heart: The City Beneath is a dark fantasy RPGs from Rowan, Rook and Decard set in a cursed underground dungeon below a mile-high city of revolution. 🎬 Trailer 🎲 Tools & Resources Heart Delve Planner - Plan out your delves and links them together to make an easy to follow map! Heart RPG FoundryVTT Package - Exactly what it says on the tin, hopefully useful for folks who need it! 📚 Useful Links Heart: The City Beneath - Delve into the nightmare dungeon Spire: The City Must Fall - Lead the revolution Rowan, Rook and Decard - Publisher’s website Want more Heart content?

item.title: Old Gods of Appalachia RPG
item.description: Welcome to the dark hollers and haunted hills of Appalachia. Based on the hit podcast, Old Gods of Appalachia brings cosmic horror to the mountains using the Cypher System. 📖 Game Review Old Gods of Appalachia Review - My full review of the game system, setting, and what makes it special 🎲 Tools & Resources Old Gods of Appalachia - Folk Magic - My thoughts on how folk magic system might work Old Gods of Appalachia - Subtle Cyphers - My attempt to make subtle cyphers for non magical characters for Old Gods.

item.title: The Magnus Archives RPG
item.description: The Magnus Archives RPG brings the hit horror podcast to life using the Cypher System. Investigate supernatural mysteries and face cosmic terrors. 🎲 Tools & Resources Coming soon! Tools and resources for The Magnus Archives are in development. 📚 Useful Links Official Magnus Archives RPG - Purchase the game The Magnus Archives Podcast - Listen to the original podcast Interested in Magnus Archives content? Let me know what you’d like to see!

item.title: Traveller RPG
item.description: Traveller is the classic science fiction RPG of exploration, trading, and adventure among the stars. 🎲 Tools & Resources Coming soon! Traveller tools and resources are in development. 📚 Useful Links Mongoose Traveller - Current edition publisher Traveller Wiki - Comprehensive Traveller resource Interested in Traveller content? Let me know what tools you need!

item.title: Shadowrun Resources Hub
item.description: Welcome to the ultimate Shadowrun resource hub! Whether you’re a GM prepping your next run or a player building your character, you’ll find everything you need right here. 🎲 Online Tools & Software Character Creation Character Generator - Beta character generator for SR2 & 3rd Edition NSRCG - Classic Shadowrun Character Generator (3rd Edition, Windows) Jackpoint - Character generator AND GM helper with tons of tools (3rd Edition, Windows 10) Matrix & Decking Matrix Builder - Detailed matrix creation for 2nd Edition Matrix Generator - Generate security sheafs on the fly (3rd Edition) Cyberdeck Calculator - Custom cyberdeck calculator (3rd Edition) Combat & Initiative Initiative Tracker - Track initiative for PCs and NPCs with condition monitors (2nd & 3rd Edition) Shadowrun Target Computer - Calculate ranged target numbers GM Tools Run Generator - Get ideas for different Shadowruns The Shop - Vehicle builder for Rigger 2 rules Valkyrie Module - Implant management system (3rd Edition) 📚 Books & Resources Purchase Books Visit the Shadowrun Books page for:

item.title: Old Gods of Appalachia Review
item.description: This is my first ever game review, I hope it is something you good folks enjoy. If so, let me know. I’d love to do a lot more of it! I have a fondness for folk magic. It’s something I end up making rules for or introducing into just about every horror game I run. Some systems have some kind of magic already. This was the case with Shadowrun and with that I’ve added what I could in new magical traditions to match other folks traditions I know about it.

item.title: Gameholecon2025
item.description: Another year another Gamehole Con in the books. This one was a lot different from my first experience because I finally stepped up to Gamemaster a bunch of games. I also worked with Monte Cook games to help show people their games and teach their system. That was an awesome experience to say the least. I also got a chance to talk to Catalyst people and got some news on our 2e battle.

item.title: Eye Witness Review
item.description: Eye Witness is an adventure for 2nd Edition Shadowrun. It is a wildly complicated backstory for a very straightforward adventure. The adventure surrounds a mystery where the players will be sent around to collect evidence for a fellow Shadowrunner bent on revenge. It will have the players bouncing all over Seattle and going to places they may have never even considered. What can the players expect? Ratings out of 5!

item.title: New Software and Approach
item.description: It’s been a little while since my last blog post, but that’s only because life has been full of exciting changes. While writing had to wait, I’ve been busy building tools and exploring new RPG projects. Today, I’m excited to share what I’ve been working on, give you some links to try them out, and even talk a bit about a possible rebrand for Null Sheen. Let’s dive in. Shadowrun 2e Foundry Prototype As with all things 2e, it is lacking a lot of great tools and platforms to make life easier for player and GMs a like due to when it came out and where software was at the time.

item.title: Shadowrun 2nd Edition Matrix 3D Prints
item.description: Before we get into this one. I have uploaded and hosted the old "Decker" game created by Shawn Overcash in 2001. It was then remade in Javascript in 2018 by Palparepa. You can find it here. Also I did land my first award from DriveThruRPG. Thank you everyone who supported my efforts! As you might have imagined, I've started leaning more into 3D prints for my tabletop adventures. There is something special about laying down custom made props that just makes everything at the table feel better.

item.title: Benefits of Physical Props for TTRPGs
item.description: Before we get into this months topic, I wanted to tell everyone I finished my next book! It can be found here: https://legacy.drivethrurpg.com/product/514075/Lone-Star-Stories-Blood-Of-The-Lamb. I’m also only 2 purchases away from getting my first Copper Badge for https://legacy.drivethrurpg.com/product/484834/Lone-Star-Stories-Blood-For-Money! If you ever wanted to support me, this would be a fantastic way to do so! Now, onto the post! Ever since I saw the original Dwarven Forge dungeons when I was a kid, I was blown away.

item.title: Double Exposure Review
item.description: “Is this the path to Eden?” - Johnathan Tang Double Exposure is an adventure for 2nd Edition Shadowrun. It is a very story heavy adventure that brings characters in a really unique situation that I haven’t seen in many other adventures. This adventure will put the player characters right in the middle of a new sprung up commune in the barons. What’s even wilder? This organization even seems to be actually growing sustainable food crops.

item.title: New Years Goals 2025
item.description: Greetings Shadowpunks! With last year behind us, I look forward to what will come in the future. This year I hope to finish two new tools for the site: Weapon Designer and Vehicle Designer. These will more than likely take at least 3 or 4 months each (Though the vehicle one will take something really special to finish in that time). My hope is always to allow players more control over their characters and to give GMs the tools to be able to create custom things quickly and easily.

item.title: Total Eclipse Review
item.description: Before we dive in! I wrote another section of my adventure: Protect and Serve. Music in the 80s was a bit more defining than today (Though Taylor Swift has definitely revived some of the old feelings of hardcore allegiance to a band or musician). Who you listened to and who you repped told you a lot about someone and in general I feel it had a lot more of a cultural impact than it does today.

item.title: Decker Problems and How to Solve Them
item.description: One of the most common problems new GMs (And old for that matter) is running the Matrix for their players. In every edition of Shadowrun, the matrix has been a pivotal part of the game. It makes sense in the larger picture, Gibson’s Neuromancer (Which you absolutely NEED to read if your running Shadowrun or Cyberpunk! [Hardcover or Sprawl Trilogy (Plus Burning Chrome) - Good Christmas Gifts!] gives us the first console cowboy and we have been seeking that experience every since.

item.title: Silver Angel Review
item.description: Silver Angel was the first published Adventure for Shadowrun. It was paired with the original Game Master’s screen for 1st Edition Shadowrun. What’s interesting is that it also came with standees (cardboard cutouts that you could use to show placement of characters and NPCs) which we wouldn’t see again until DMZ was released. These paired excellently with the Sprawl Maps. This adventure is pretty short, but you can see the gritty bones of what would become the Shadowrun we all know and love.

item.title: Queen Euphoria Review Notes
item.description: It seems I am often drawn to Shadowrun adventures with famous characters in the fiction of the world. Starting with Dark Angel and now moving onto Queen Euphoria, there is something special about the world that was written in those first few adventures of Shadowrun. Queen Euphoria is one of the classic adventures that in general is one of the few truly horror based adventures in Shadowrun. Given there are always horror elements in all adventures in Shadowrun.

item.title: Borrowing Rules for Better Games
item.description: A lot of tabletops have attempts to make the perfect system. This of course has lent to tons of iterations of different ways of rolling dice, creating statistics and skill tests. This however has the amazing byproduct of creating some mechanics that are system agnostic that you can use to make your games more exciting and more interesting regardless of the game you are running. Here are 6 examples from games that are near and dear to my heart.

item.title: Gamehole Con Review
item.description: Cons and I have a kind of strange relationship. In general, I absolutely love meeting people but I get very concerned with large crowds. So Gamehole turned out to be just about the perfect size for me. Plenty of people but easy enough to walk around without having to struggle through a crowd. One of the main concerns with a “smaller con” is that its harder to get big names to show up to the cons.

item.title: Anchoring Foci and Spells and Why You Should Be Using Them More Often
item.description: When we play games with magic often we look at it in the lens of our understanding most of us learned from games like Dungeons and Dragons. That is to say that magical people always go into combat with magical shields and energy blasts. Fireballs and Counterspells to keep yourself from getting fried. Shadowrun has a lot of this too, but there is more insidious magic that exists and it’s as old as the human race.

item.title: Fancy Program Name
item.description: Program Names for more immersion I’ve always wanted to sit down and write down some fun program names for decking programs. Having programs with ratings is one of the things that made older editions of Shadowrun infinitely more fun. This is an attempt to make something more interesting for folks. Let me know if this is something folks enjoy and if they would want more of them! Analyze Program Suite Rating Name Rating 1 Preliminary Scan Mk1 Rating 2 Insight Probe V2 Rating 3 DataSight Recon 3.

item.title: Running Divided Assets in Cyberpunk Red
item.description: Most of you know me as someone who writes things for Shadowrun and Cyberpunk concepts in general. I currently am playing Cyberpunk Red just as often as playing Shadowrun 3rd edition. So I begin to see the tying threads between concepts and stories. A lot of stories in Shadowrun cannot be ported to Cyberpunk. The fantasy element is played pretty often in these stories, but every once in a while it’s left almost entirely out.

item.title: Divided Assets Review
item.description: I know it’s been awhile folks! I’ve been busy writing Holostreets! Blood For Money and Blood on the Pavement. I am looking for people to review these in any capacity so if you are interested please reach out to me! Otherwise, lets get into Divided Assets! Divided Assets is a Shadowrun adventure for 2nd Edition. The plot revolves around kidnapping and the ramifications of extractions on the family of those being extracted.

item.title: Dark Angel Review
item.description: Dark Angel is a Shadowrun adventure for 2nd Edition. The plot revolves around recovering the original recordings from the Legendary Rocker, Dark Angel. Seems he killed himself (though it’s murky that anyone would set himself on fire as a way of going out) and some new kid suddenly showed up with recordings and signed contracts. This of course seems like the HOTTEST of Bull Drek that could be, because Angel swore he would never record them and sell out.

item.title: Older Cyberpunk Tech Movies
item.description: I’ve always found it so interesting that so many movies out there feel like the framework for what became Shadowrun and Cyberpunk. I know the zeitgeist of the time was that technology was going to give us a world that would allow for infinite convenience and automation. That would have allowed tech to come into every aspect of our lives, from micro foods that give us all of the nutrients needed to cars that could beat traffic by flying into the sky.

item.title: Vtt Maps and Shadowrun Info
item.description: After doing some digging and trying to find some good resources for VTT maps (A question that I see gets posted all over the place). I also needed a bit of a lighter post as I finish up the final touches on “Blood On the Pavement”, my next release for the Lonestar Stories first season. This should be available soon! Please take a look at my first releases: Blood For Money!

item.title: Magical Traditions Part 3 Santeria
item.description: Books/Sites Used in this article. Magic In The Shadows SR3 Core aboutsanteria.com - Vocab For Santeria Images are used from Creative Common Licenses. As we forge ahead in new paths of magic in the world we come to another tradition that fall outside of the UCAS and CAS control for the majority of their practitioners. Yoruba in this cases. This faith has similar bone structures of Voodoo and Palo Mayombe. With powerful spirits being called to in order to effect change and offerings to be left and honor to be given.

item.title: Bottle Demon - Review and Tweaks
item.description: Overview: Bottled Demon is a Shadowrun adventure written for Shadowrun 1st Edition. The premise of the adventure revolved around a local fixer needing muscle to ensure a deal goes down nice and clean. What he has is worth some serious nuyen and he is willing to pay out a good deal of cred to make sure he is safe while finding a buyer. This is a very magic heavy adventure with room for almost nothing else.

item.title: Cyberpunk Stl Files for Props
item.description: STL Files for Shadowrun and Cyberpunk (Red or otherwise) So my table is pretty extreme when it comes to making props and cityscapes. We just love being drawn in by massive 3d print builds and honestly, once you know where to look, it mostly comes down to time. The cost of these things is pretty tiny in the long run. $100 of filament can go a LONG freaking way. So I wanted to put together a list of stuff we used to print out the mega city that we play in.

item.title: What Shadowrun Edition Should I Choose
item.description: One of the most common questions we get on the various Shadowrun Subreddits is what edition is right for me and my group? Think is unfortunately always answered with the obvious “It depends”. I’m going to hopefully lay out all the information to make the choice for you and your group. Each edition has a lot to offer and gives a completely different feel to your game. Let’s see where your group falls.

item.title: Product Review - Enhance Travel Bag 'Adventurer'
item.description: Enhance Travel Bag Adventurer I have never written one of these product reviews, but as I understand it, this is how you make a successful blog: finding the best of the items for those who would actually use them. I thought perhaps I would start with something I use pretty often. So let’s talk about the “ENHANCE Travel Bag” I’m one of those people who have to own the books. I get that people love PDFs, but for me there is nothing better than a new gaming book for something I am running.

item.title: The Differences Between Cyberpunk and Shadowrun
item.description: In the vast and complex landscape of cyberpunk fiction, two prominent universes have captured the imaginations of fans worldwide: Cyberpunk and Shadowrun. While both exist within the gritty, neon-lit framework of cyberpunk, they diverge significantly in their themes, with Cyberpunk focusing on legacy, and Shadowrun focusing on the living. I hope to highlight how each universe uniquely defines the essence of its characters and the worlds they inhabit and why this matters to you and your game.

item.title: Top Five Cyberpunk Movies
item.description: Sometimes you just need a movie to get your Cyberpunk on. What better way than to catch some of the best Cyberpunk movies out there. Anyone running Shadowrun should have at least seen these movies mentioned in order to get some insight into the minds and feelings surrounding the game as its inception. Some of these are newer movies but based on much older properties. What is Cyberpunk? This is a question that is often asked when it comes to trying to wrap your head around the Cyberpunk Genre.

item.title: What Should I Play in Shadowrun?
item.description: Books Useful for this article. SR1 Core - PDF SR2 Core SR3 Core - PDF SR4 Core - PDF SR5 Core SR6 Core - PDF This question is more common for new players than most GMs realize. Without classes and sets of specific dynamics to choose from, some players (especially those from games like Dungeons and Dragons) have a hard time understanding what kind of character to put together and that makes character creation overall much more difficult.

item.title: Shadowrun Books
item.description: Looking for a specific Shadowrun Book? Wanting to add some books to your collection? I’ve put together as many books as I can scouring Amazon and Noble Knight to help you get what you are looking for. These are affiliate links, so buying items through this page does help me. So I thank you in advanced if you do end up finding something. I’ll be continuing to add to this page as I find new stuff I think is interesting and worth your time.

item.title: Unusual Rewards for Shadowruns
item.description: Books Used in this article. SR3 Core Mr. Johnson’s Little Black Book Shadowrun is a game about high risk and high rewards. Too often we however only see rewards of nuyen. Some Gamemasters stretch their legs and add in things like extra contacts or some exotic ammos, but when was the last time you had something like a program, spell or exotic material for resale? Each of those rewards can lead to more stories and more stories is ALWAYS better!

item.title: Spells - Buying, Learning and Forms
item.description: Books Useful for this article. Magic In The Shadows SR3 Core Street Wyld One of the things a lot of people may overlook in Shadowrun is how spells are purchased/learned in game. I think it’s very important to understand that mages and shamans need to get access to either teachers or buying formulas. This can also be an excellent opportunity to bring in new NPCs and teachers. In most games I see a lot of people just spend the karma and poof!

item.title: Affliation and Moving Forward
item.description: After doing a lot of thinking about this little side project of mine, I had decided I perhaps needed to begin to supplement the cost of this website/hosting for tools/investment of time. I hate to make any kind of clutter or cause anyone any strife when it comes to reading my articles, using my tools, or just generally enjoying the spirit of the reason my site exists. However, seeing the cost continue to rise, I will need to come up with something.

item.title: Magical Traditions in Your Game: Part 2
item.description: Books Used in this article. Magic In The Shadows SR3 Core As my introduction to magical traditions seems to have gathered a good deal of attention, I thought I would continue on with this. As always, if people have specific things they want covered, please send me an email, or reach out to me on Discord, and I’ll be more than happy to sit down and write something up for you.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description: PRIVACY NOTICE Last updated May 09, 2024 This privacy notice for NullSheen.com ("we," “us,” or “our”), describes how and why we might collect, store, use, and/or share ("process") your information when you use our services ("Services"), such as when you: Visit our website at https://www.nullsheen.com, or any website of ours that links to this privacy notice Engage with us in other related ways, including any sales, marketing, or events Questions or concerns?

item.title: About Me
item.description: About Dean Professional Programmer & Shadowrun Enthusiast Hello! I’m Dean, a seasoned programmer with nearly a decade of experience crafting dynamic web solutions using PHP, JavaScript, and Python. I earned my stripes in Web Development from Full Sail University, where I honed my skills and developed a deep understanding of the digital landscape. Programming is more than just a job for me; it’s a platform that allows me to blend my technical skills with my personal passions.

item.title: Contact
item.description: Are you passionate about digital preservation? Do you possess software tools that once played a pivotal role at your table or other creative endeavors you want preserved? If so, I’m eager to connect with you. My mission is to archive essential tools before they vanish into history. Along this journey, I’ve met several individuals who share this vision. They are the custodians of software that, though no longer available, once laid the groundwork for innovation and eased the burdens of GMs.

item.title: Magical Traditions in Your Game: Part 1
item.description: Books Used in this article. Magic In The Shadows SR3 Core I’ve always been drawn to various faiths as I grew up. What people believed and why was always so interesting to me. Being able to walk into a new church, temple or holy place and seeing the customs, hearing the music and enjoying lessons the faiths had. I tend to gravitate towards character concepts that have faith as their foundation.

item.title: Custom Shadowrun 2nd Edition Sheets for Roll20
item.description: As we continue to march forward in reclaiming our past a few people stepped up and created tools for our future. One such person is Lord Nago. He has created several pieces for a 2nd Edition Shadowrun Roll20 setup. With his permission i am making this post and hosting these files for him. Feel free to reach out to him on Reddit if you have questions or requests! Here is his original post on Reddit: https://www.

item.title: Shadowrun 3rd Edition - Errata
item.description: This is a scrape from the https://www.shadowrunrpg.com site for ALL of their Erratas. They seem to have kind of let this go to seed and I know a TON of people are looking for this. I take no claim for any of this, I just want to preserve it for future generations to have the corrections they might be looking for. Shadowrun 3rd Edition Errata Version 4.1 Date: 02/28/2004 All changes are reflected in the corrected thirteenth printing of Shadowrun, Third Edition (the fourth printing by FanPro).

item.title: Ghostbusters RPG With Shadowrun 3rd Edition
item.description: The New Ghost Busters - RPG using Shadowrun 3rd Edition Around 20 years ago I fell in love with a franchise I thought was dead. It was funny, it was spooky and it was wierd. I was never the same after seeing Ghostbusters for the first time. I too wanted to hold back the darkness with my wit and genius. Fighting with the heart of a lion against what goes bump in the night.

item.title: Better Chase Scenes
item.description: Vehicle Chases Vehicle chases have always been a thorn in my side since I started playing Shadowrun. I’ve always wanted that cinematic experience of the PCs’ vehicles skittering down a road while gangers chase them, dragging chains through the streets. This has never been really successfully done. So many attempts, so many times I tried to figure out the chase TNs. So this is my attempt to fix it so you can make chase TNs and Scenes that will make your players sit on the edge of their seat.

item.title: VTT Icons and Resources
item.description: As we spoke about before, Covid really threw our lives when it came to game into some strangeness. We had to spin up new methods of keeping our stories alive. Though this is my least favorite of the ways to run a game. It holds itself aloft as something that can bring people together from across the world to enjoy the stories of friends who cannot gather around a table. Though in a more modern adaptation.

item.title: Neo Anarchist's Guide to Everything Else
item.description: We have another Zine that blazed trails in the Shadowrun universe! Started by Jerry Stratton and continued by Tony Moller. This along with the Shadowrun Supplemental were among the first player published materials out there. The first issue came out in 1992 and the last in 1994. As these are free to distribute I wanted to ensure it had a place among the archive of everything else Shadowrun had to offer for those who may have missed the boat so to speak on these wonderful publications.

item.title: The Shadowrun Supplementals
item.description: On January 1st, 1992 we were given Shadowrun in its second form. Now revised with an attempt to bring the systems together and resolve some rules disputes, this gave us the form Shadowrun would exist in for some time. In 1997 Adam Jury rounded up some articles and put together the first Shadowrun Supplemental. This was a collection of articles written by players and Game Masters about topics they wanted to see in their games.

item.title: Character Sheets
item.description: After doing some digging into character sheets for an up and coming game. I suddenly realize the crap shoot that was finding character sheets for Shadowrun. On one hand, it makes sense. The IP has been shared and scattered to the winds across half a dozen companies all keeping their own digital presence. On the other, we used to have things like dumpshock.org to keep us with what we needed. Nowadays, well it’s a whole different ball game.

item.title: Digital Tools for a Better Game
item.description: As time continued forward and with the horrid times that was the panic following the COVID, we as tabletop gamers ran into some serious problems. The games that we wanted to play with our friends suddenly had to move online. Separated from our friends and unable to share a table, we turned to the tools that were made for online play. The real problem here is, most things were made for DnD.

item.title: How to be a Decker including Dumb Matrix Tricks
item.description: Books Used in this article. Matrix Target: Matrix SR3 Core When you have a decker in the group, a lot of people miss some golden opportunities. Deckers can add great resources outside of opening security doors and stopping poison gas from geeking your team. Part of it is realizing how much cyber crime your decker can do for you. This usually comes in the form of a few common decking activites.

item.title: Matrix Example - Doc Wagon
item.description: Books Used in this article. Matrix Target: Matrix SR3 Core As we continue with these examples we are going to continue to add more complexities to these systems. In this system we are going to introduce multiple nodes. Each node itself will have its own security sheaf. Each system will be marked with a letter and its Sheaf will be added in a table next to its letter. A new rule you will need to pick up as well is that security tally carries as the decker moves between different subsystems.

item.title: Matrix System Examples - Grid Guide
item.description: Books Used in this article. Matrix Target: Matrix SR3 Core A lot of cyberpunk revolves around crazy new technology. In almost every system imaginable, this includes some kind of virtual reality system that people have become obsessed with. Part of this is due to the escape it offers. A paradise outside of the nightmare society has wrought with shifting laws to allow for hyper capitalism. Regardless of why, it leads to characters who want to enter this world and most importantly, take on the counter cultural heroes known as Hackers, Deckers, Phreakers or the likes.

item.title: Lonestar Adventure - Coding
item.description: Books Useful for this article. SR2 Core Lone Star This post uses my Lonestar Campaign Setting found here. Adventure Title: Coding Adventure Type: Cyber Crime Setting: Seattle, 2075 Plot Overview: The group of officers arrive on scene of a catastrophic traffic accident (Go Gang related attack with civilians caught in the crossfire). The EMTs arrive after the officers give what aid they can. One of the victims has an incredibly rare blood type and is an organ donor (this rare type is resistant to organ growth, so vat grown organs are off the table) and needs to be rushed to the nearest hospital (with police escort).

item.title: Shadowrun Podcasts
item.description: I’ve been looking to begin cataloging even more Shadowrun resources for folks so this site becomes a one stop shop. If you have a podcast you want added to the list, just reach out to me with the details and i’ll be sure to add you! Actual Play Podcasts Title URL Description Tags Pink Fohawk Spotify 2nd Edition, Pink M(F)ohawk, SFX Added, Raunchy Paydata Spotify BG Music, 5th Edition SINless Spotify 3th Edition and 4th Edition NeoScum Spotify 4th Edition, SFX Added, Comedy, Crude Humour City of Thorns Spotify Needs Description Resting Glitch Face Spotify Anarchy and 6th Edition, Comedy Shadowrun Origins Libsyn Classic Runs from Shadowrun, 5th Edition (but using classic Matrix setups) The Arcology Podcast Website Needs Description Pride Against Prejudice Spotify 4th Edition As the 6th World Turns Twitch 2nd Edition Fixer Files Podcast Addict Mostly Anarchy, Some 5th Edition It’s a Bug Not a Feature Spotify 6th Edition Bug City Blues Spotify 6th Edition Tales from the Stuffer Shack Podchaser Needs Description Critical Glitch Libsyn Inactive, but still downloadable Berlin - It’s a Bug not a ferature Spotify In German, 6th Edition Burning Edge Spotify What the Dice Spotify Without a Net Spotify The Dragon Variation Spotify Crit Squad Spotify Deniable Assets Spotify In-Character, Audio Drama Relative Dimension Podbean Shadowrun, Earthdawn and more!

item.title: Decker Game
item.description: A long long time in a galaxy far far away, any kind of shadowrun content was gold. It was rare and beautiful to behold. Decker was one of those nuggets from the era. It was difficult, but getting ahead was extremely satisfying. This of course led to save scumming like never before! All of this said, it was written in an era of windows that has since passed, but just like everything these days.

item.title: Lonestar Adventure - Blood for Money
item.description: Books Useful for this article. SR2 Core Lone Star This post uses my Lonestar Campaign Setting found here. Adventure Title: Blood for Money Adventure Type: Kidnapping Setting: Seattle, 2075 Plot Overview: Brendon Evers thinks he has a perfect plan to finally get out from under his parents thumb. However his two friends (Eddy and Simon) let slip to their more dangerous street friend Kyle, who has an idea to make everyone rich (except Brendon of course, he will be dead).

item.title: Lonestar Game
item.description: Adventures using this setting Blood For Money Coding Lone Star Campaign For a long time i’ve had a desire to run a Lone Star game. I’ve always been a huge fan of police procedural’s on TV and I thought i’d take a stab a writing a small campaign for it. I then had the idea of releasing these as episodic posts someone might be able to use for their own campaign.

item.title: Software And Tools
item.description: I’ve decided to attempt to collect as much as I possibly can for various projects. I’ll keep updating this as I find more tools. Some of these may not work anymore (As most were made for Windows 3.x and 95). If anyone knows of any others, please reach out to me at Dean@ this domain! I can’t wait to hear from you. My Tools Tool URL Edition Description Character Generator https://character-generator.

item.title: Old Sr2 Character Generator
item.description: After doing a deep dive on researching old Shadowrun Programs (and I think I’ve decided to collect them all for mirrors) I stumbled onto an SR2 Character Creator. This was great for me because if I end up going down that road of making a character creator, I now have all of the base data files to extract the data and put it into a standard format (JSON in this case).

item.title: Initiative Tracker
item.description: After some more requests for tools, I bring you all my initiative tracker. So far it is made for Shadowrun 2nd And 3rd Edition (With a simple toggle for each edition). Currently it is configured to instantly remove initiative as they take damage. This is how my table plays however I realize a lot of others may not. I will make a toggle in the future which will remove this feature for tables that don’t play with it.

item.title: Data Preservation Shadowrun 3rd
item.description: After seeing various sites I used to rely on disappearing day by day, I have grown nostalgic about the various programs i had used through out the years. As I have seen new players dig for resources for them to come up empty it has sparked my own interest in tossing my hat into the ring. I of course cannot promise I will suceed in some of these plans that seeing this data has given me, but what I can promise, is getting the data into a place for it to be preserved for the future.

item.title: Things That Need to Be Around
item.description: There are a lot of tools I used growing up and trying to learn Shadowrun. The few that always made me happiest were the ones that really gave me the fully control and customization that Shadowrun allows. People at FASA really dug into the idea that characters will want signature weapons/vehicles/spells/etc. That being a major part of who they are meant we needed that DEEP control over who and what they were.

item.title: Pink Fohawk - Shadowrun Podcast
item.description: When I started Shadowrun again, I wanted to be able to hear more stories from different people in order to get back into the mindset of it. I stumbled on Pink Fohawk and found another few folks who are deeply passionate about Shadowrun. Their podcast is incredibly fun, it has some folly and music work, but it definitely just some friends sitting around a table and having a damn good time.

item.title: Archive
item.description: As I continue on with this site, I stumble across all kinds of things I want archived. I hope that this archive out survives me and that we can preserve things that would once be just a figment in someones memory for those who have yet to pick up a Shadowrun book. This is all done in that effort, to make a preserved snap shot of the world as we knew it when we first began our own journeys.

item.title: Shadowrun 3rd Edition - Matrix Security Sheaf Generator
item.description: Books Used in this article. Matrix Target: Matrix SR3 Core Awhile ago we had need for a good security generator. I know there have been quite a few, and I checked on an old one that I used to use, and the hosting was going out on it. I took a lot of the concepts and rolled them into a new React project. It goes a bit above and beyond for it most of what was there.

item.title: Site Launched
item.description: Starting Fresh After doing a lot of game play for a group of friends and getting back into the community at large. I (and some friends) have decided to make a space for tools and things we have ended up writing for the Shadowrun (2nd and 3rd) systems. This will start out pretty small, but hopefully will have a lot of helpful stuff for people in need for tools for randomization or just in need of being able to put together systems for their games.

item.title: Shadowrun STLs
item.description: Miniatures Title URL Description Cost Dwarf With Crazy Gun! https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4716824 Free Cyberpunk https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4163741 Male Character Free Cowgirl gunslinger https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:5548964 Free 6 different cyberpunk style women https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4092668 Free 6 different cyberpunk style men https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4092684 Free Rocker girl sniper https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4075869 Free Robots for Cyberpunk! https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4092671 Free Generic Cyberpunk Girl https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4191425 Free Rocker Girl Dual Guns https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4073045 Free Leaper for Cyberpunk / Ghouls for Shadowrun https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:4092676 Free Female Decker/Netrunner with Drone https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:3650803 Free Street People https://www.
